operation_list.reason: Lamp obj_1 rests on left nightstand obj_8 (on_top_of G3; object_state stacking base=obj_8 top=[obj_1]); confirmed visually in reference.
Bag obj_13 rests on right nightstand obj_7 (on_top_of G4; object_state stacking base=obj_7).
Book obj_14 rests on right nightstand obj_7 (on_top_of G4; object_state stacking base=obj_7).
Force floor-grounding right nightstand obj_7 (object_state attached_to=floor; class nightstand).
Force floor-grounding left nightstand obj_8 (object_state attached_to=floor; class nightstand).
Window obj_12 mounted on back wall (attached_to=wall); nearest wall Wall_03; matches two side-by-side windows on the back wall in reference.

Picture frame obj_2 (upper-right of reference) appears front-facing/un-foreshortened, consistent with the back wall (Wall_03) rather than the right wall (Wall_02); distance also favors Wall_03 (0.455 vs 0.689). Kept Wall_03.
Picture frame obj_3 (upper-left of reference) mounted on the left wall (Wall_04); nearest wall 0.353m; matches reference.
Window obj_9 mounted on back wall (attached_to=wall); nearest wall Wall_03; flush wall-attach also pulls it back out of the bed, resolving the obj_4<->obj_9 collision.

review_notes: Carried forward all 9 heuristic ops unchanged; no deletions and no wall reassignments were needed. Ambiguous picture frame obj_2 (candidates Wall_03/Wall_02): the upper-right framed picture in the reference appears front-facing rather than foreshortened, which matches the back wall (Wall_03); distance also favored Wall_03 (0.455 vs 0.689), so Wall_03 was kept. Same-wall window pair G2 (obj_9, obj_12) was inspected for size consistency: z-spread is fine (0.026 m), but the scale-spread is large because the two windows use fundamentally different meshes (base widths 2.57 m vs 0.77 m). Aligning the uniform scale parameter would NOT equalize their visual size and risks corrupting proportions, so update_size was intentionally NOT emitted; the flush wall-attach (preserve_rotation=False) additionally seats obj_9 back against Wall_03 and resolves its protrusion-collision with the bed (obj_4). Ghost-duplicate scan: obj_7/obj_8 (~2.3 m apart) and obj_9/obj_12 (~0.68 m apart) are genuinely distinct pairs, not duplicates. Off-axis-facing, missing-support, and wall-discovery scans found no gaps: lamp->obj_8 and bag/book->obj_7 supports are already covered, no additional wall-mounted items are visible, and the bed mass (obj_4, captured under class 'pillow') already rests at floor level. No new vision-evident ops required.
